title: Lodor config.json
description: THE single source of truth for the on-device config.json read by BOTH the Go engine (engine/config/config.go) AND every launcher/integration that parses it (lodoros/launcher/minui.c, the integration paks). Any reader on any side MUST conform to this nesting. The C launcher reads connection fields from hosts[0] — NOT from top level. The nested-vs-flat divergence is exactly what gated the LODOR card to onboarding (root_uri/token/device_id live under hosts[0], the launcher checked top-level).

hosts.description: RomM servers. hosts[0] is the active host. Connection identity (root_uri, token, device_id) lives HERE, not at top level.

device_id.description: this device identity; REQUIRED for the launcher connected-gate to pass

tier.description: endpoint preference rank, LOWER wins (#87/#84): 1=Tailscale internal (preferred), 2=public Cloudflare Access (fallback); absent/0 = legacy single endpoint. Engine-only; the launcher ignores it.

socks5_proxy.description: tier-1 ONLY: local SOCKS5 proxy host:port (e.g. localhost:1055) the engine dials with REMOTE DNS to reach a *.ts.net MagicDNS RomM host through userspace tailscaled (#84). Engine-only.

cf_access.description: tier-2 ONLY: Cloudflare Access service-token pair sent as CF-Access-Client-* headers IN ADDITION to the RomM bearer. client_secret is a SECRET — never logged/committed.

directory_mappings.description: platform folder name -> {slug, relative_path}; written by the mirror.
